hero.title: Preserving Endangered Languages
hero.subtitle: A digital platform dedicated to documenting and preserving the world's endangered languages through written documents, vocabulary databases, and audio recordings.
hero.title_language: {{language}} has been spoken for over a thousand years.
hero.subtitle_language: We're documenting it before it isn't.

hero_v2.eyebrow: A language falls silent every two weeks
hero_v2.sub: Nativo documents the world's endangered languages — their vocabulary, their writing, and the voices that speak them. Every word you add to {{language}} keeps it alive a little longer.
hero_v2.reassure: Free, open, and built by communities like yours.

hero_card.title: Look up a word in {{language}}
hero_card.hint: Search the dictionary, or watch words drift by while you think.
hero_card.no_entry_body: This word hasn't been documented in {{language}}. You could be the first to add it.

dictionary.subtitle_with_target: Search {{source}} vocabulary and see {{target}} translations. Change the target via the language picker in the header.
dictionary.empty: No matches for “{{query}}” yet. Contributors are still building this dictionary.
dictionary.rate_limited: Too many requests — please slow down and try again in a minute.

activity.heading: Recent activity in {{language}}
activity.empty: Nothing yet — be the first to add a word, document, or recording.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.document_body: Record and preserve written documents in endangered languages
mission.vocabulary_body: Create comprehensive databases with translations and pronunciations
mission.audio_body: Store authentic recordings of native speakers
mission.share_body: Make linguistic heritage accessible for future generations

spotlight.empty: No words yet — be the first to add one.

endangerment.headline: UNESCO classifies {{language}} as {{level}}.
endangerment.progress: {{count}} of an estimated {{target}} core words documented.
endangerment.body_bar: In rural areas children still grow up with it; in and around the cities, increasingly not.
endangerment.body_umu: The youngest fluent speakers are in their seventies and older.
endangerment.body_haw: A revitalization movement is underway; fluent native speakers remain few.
endangerment.body_nav: Still the most widely spoken Indigenous language in North America, but younger speakers are declining.
endangerment.body_ain: Only a handful of fully fluent speakers remain, most very elderly.
endangerment.body_eng: Spoken by all generations across many regions; not classified as endangered.

audio_page.title: {{language}} audio
audio_page.subtitle: Recordings linked to words in this language.
audio_page.empty_hint: Audio upload is on the roadmap — recordings will appear here once contributors add them.

contributors_page.title: {{language}} contributors
contributors_page.subtitle: Everyone who has joined or contributed to this language.
contributors_page.empty: No contributors for this language yet.

learn.heading: Learn {{language}}
learn.subtitle: Read your way in — each text builds on the words you already know.
learn.guest_banner: You can read everything right now. Create an account to track the words you know and get the right next text picked for you.
learn.guest_banner_cta: Sign up to keep your place
learn.load_failed: Failed to load the learning path.
learn.empty: No texts are ready for learning in {{language}} yet.
learn.empty_hint: A text joins the path once its words are fully linked to the dictionary. Editors: finish linking a short text and it will appear here.
learn.step_no_new_words: No new words — a comfortable read

reader.no_text: This document has no readable text yet.
reader.tap_hint: Tap any word to see what it means.
reader.tap_hint_member: Tap any word to see what it means — that also tells us which words to keep practising with you.
reader.saved: Progress saved — your next text will match how this one felt.
reader.guest_cta_post: to track the words you know and get the right next text.
reader.diff_easy_hint: Give me more new words next time
